text: was wondering what  you thought of a microwave piston engine on a microbial scale. picture the worlds  smallest  v120000000 engine  all run by tiny thermometers super sensitive to.. "static"

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: The tpu is something i am very interested in too - i will follow your project with interest - thanks for the heads up - keep up the good work.

author: @theicediamond7
text: i am very interested in tpu bruce. out of all the force releasing projects this has promise. the tpu was put together by a man with nothing but dogged drive. check it out this is my next project.

author: @iluzyon1
text: Hey :) thank you sir! I appreciate that. I always loved flash lights and been facinated by electronic circuits. I tried it for the first time maybe 8 or 10 months ago and i am addicted now lolll. Most of my circuits are replicas but it am learning a lot from them.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: I think you are right - iluzyon seems to be suggesting the same thing with his idea of leaving some space between the spark plates and the plunger. I'll build it in the next revision and we can see what happens!

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: incidentally - haven't mentioned it but - i love you work on joule thiefs - i must try and get some time to play around with the things you are doing - very impressive

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: My thoughts exactly - i have started constructing one - it should be finished tomorrow. I am also going to try the capacitors arranged in a cockcroft walton arrangement and see what happens - should be interesting

author: @theicediamond7
text: the water got in the way of the force expanding out. it did not explode

author: @iluzyon1
text: Lolll cool. The plunger is not going far but the principle works good. Throwing another idea here, A completely closed (sealed) cylinder at the bottom plus a really snug plunger at the top of cylindre, leaving some space between the spark plates and the plunger. I do think it would go real high :)  Anyways, good work!
